title: Learned soft regime-switching ensemble instead of hand-built regime keys
summary: The best code already does regime-based blending, which is a good sign, but it uses fixed bins and shrinkage heuristics.
rationale: The best code already does regime-based blending, which is a good sign, but it uses fixed bins and shrinkage heuristics. State-space tyre modeling suggests structural resets and changing latent pace states; a learned gate can exploit that more flexibly than static `early/mid/late × tyre_bin × pos_bin` buckets.
implementation_hint: Train 3-5 specialized experts on disjoint slices such as dry-normal, wet/intermediate, early-stint, old-tyre, and late-race pressure. Then fit a tiny fold-local gate (logistic regression or shallow booster) on observable state features only (`RaceProgress`, `TyreLife`, `Compound`, `Stint`, `LapTime_Delta`, `Position`) to predict which expert’s OOF rank is most reliable, and use the gate to soft-blend test predictions.
expected_effect: More adaptive than fixed regime weights, especially if current heuristics are averaging away sharp phase changes like wet tyres, fresh-stop resets, or end-of-stint cliffs.
risk: Mixture gates can overfit very easily if trained on raw scores without strict OOF discipline; benefit may vanish if experts are too correlated.
